title: USD Document Data Model Capability
purpose: Define layer/spec/field storage behavior for the bounded document-model subset used by single-layer USDA parsing.

included: prim specs addressed by absolute prim paths
attribute and relationship specs addressed by property paths
spec field maps with typed field values exposed as canonical tagged values only at adapter boundaries
field existence, stored type inspection, and checked typed reads
native numeric payload storage for numeric field values
complete foundational typed payload catalog preservation inside field values
root and nested prim child-list maintenance
active field preservation without layer-level filtering
deferred: variant set and variant spec storage
complete core metadata field validation table
typed validation for every specialized core field
composition, value resolution, asset resolution, and stage population
schema fallback evaluation and schema-domain field semantics

required.reason: Field names and repeated child/property names should use token identity rather than raw string identity.
Spec names and field names are document-model identifiers.
Field storage consumes the foundational typed value model and exposes canonical tagged values only at adapter boundaries.
Spec identity and child insertion are addressed through canonical prim and property paths.
Document-model fields may store list operations, including specialized ObjectPath list operations.

field_value_model.representation: durable storage uses a target-native FieldValue or equivalent typed value container, not JSON as the primary representation
field_value_model.adapter_representation: canonical tagged JSON values matching contracts/value.schema.json are used at conformance and serialization boundaries
field_value_model.json_materialization_rule: Tagged JSON objects must be generated on demand for adapters or serialization and must not be retained as durable field payloads.
field_value_model.typed_read_rule: Document-model field lookup exposes field existence, stored type identity, and checked typed reads without materializing or reparsing adapter JSON. A read succeeds only when the requested type agrees with the stored field value type; otherwise it reports a type mismatch.
field_value_model.storage_shape_rule: The physical field value storage shape is implementation-defined and may be std::variant, tagged union, enum-plus-payload storage, arena-owned values, or a backend-native value object.
field_value_model.numeric_storage_rule: Numeric field values use native numeric payloads inherited from the foundational value model. Decimal strings are permitted only at text/JSON serialization boundaries and must not be parsed back for typed field reads or composition helper queries.
field_value_model.foundational_payload_catalog_rule: Field values that carry foundational data preserve the complete foundational payload catalog: scalar domain and width/precision, dimensioned element type and shape, matrix order and shape, quaternion component order, semantic alias role and underlying type, homogeneous array element metadata, dictionary typed entries, and valueBlock sentinel identity. A document-model backend may use a common storage container, but typed field reads and composition helpers must observe those distinctions without reparsing serialized JSON.
field_value_model.metadata_alias_rule: Metadata fields must store underlying foundational types, not semantic aliases. Attribute typeName may name semantic aliases separately from stored values.
field_value_model.listops: List operations are stored as field values supplied by usd-listops-authored. Specialized listOp<Reference> and listOp<Payload> fields retain native arc records internally and materialize asset/path strings only when serialized.

specialized_field_values.specifier: native enum/domain value for def, over, or class; not a durable string payload
specialized_field_values.variability: native enum/domain value for varying or uniform; not a durable string payload
specialized_field_values.Retiming: native layer-offset record with offset and scale numeric members
specialized_field_values.Relocates: native map from source PathRef to target PathRef, where an empty target remains an empty path reference
specialized_field_values.TimeSamples: native map keyed by numeric time ordinates whose values are FieldValue/value-block entries, not string-keyed JSON
specialized_field_values.Spline: native spline record retaining curve type, extrapolation, loop, knot, tangent, interpolation, and custom-data authoring

field_value_model.token_storage_rule: Tagged token and token[] values must be represented internally by the target's token identity capability or an equivalent atomized identity mechanism. String spellings may appear at parser and JSON adapter boundaries.

field_identity_model.representation: metadata field names are field-name tokens, not durable raw string keys
field_identity_model.extension_fields: Unknown extension metadata fields are validated as field names and interned into the same field-name token identity domain.

laws: each spec path is unique within a layer
the layer spec always exists at /
field map keys use token identity internally while preserving canonical field-name spellings at serialization boundaries

domain-facing document-model operations use token values for token-typed fields such as prim typeName and for reusable spec/property names; parser and JSON adapters may intern from strings before calling the domain API
domain-facing document-model operations use path handles for spec identity; parser and JSON adapters may parse path strings before calling the domain API

path parsing, stringification, and parent/name path construction are owned by usd-paths; document-model Layer APIs consume and return path handles and must not expose Layer-owned parse/stringify operations
the known core field vocabulary is pre-interned as field tokens; textual spellings are seed data and serialization output, not the durable field identity

generated parsers should use pre-interned known core field tokens for known fields such as primOrder, propertyOrder, payload, references, typeName, defaultPrim, documentation, and targetPaths rather than resolving those names through extension-field string lookup
generated parsers should classify authored metadata spellings and aliases such as doc->documentation before storage, so known fields never enter the extension field-token path
durable field storage uses a FieldValue-like model capable of representing foundational values and specialized field values; JSON is an interchange format, not the primary storage abstraction
field lookup exposes existence and stored type identity without requiring canonical JSON materialization
typed field reads return the requested payload only when the requested type agrees with the stored field value type; otherwise they report a type mismatch
numeric field payloads are native numeric values rather than stringified numbers

foundational field payloads preserve the complete foundational typed payload catalog, including half, timecode, vector, quaternion, matrix, semantic alias, homogeneous array, dictionary, and valueBlock categories
document-model fields must not collapse dimensioned, matrix, quaternion, or semantic alias values into untyped arrays for durable storage or typed reads
the document-model contract does not prescribe the physical field value storage layout

specifier, variability, Retiming, Relocates, TimeSamples, and Spline field values must be target-native domain values or equivalent compact variants, not plain strings, Python dict/list payloads, std::map<string, Json>, or adapter JSON trees
durable field storage must not retain adapter JSON payloads for unmodeled values; use a native payload/value tree and materialize JSON only on output
token-valued metadata and token[] child/name-list metadata use token identity internally while preserving authored spellings at serialization boundaries
adding a root prim appends its name to layer primChildren
adding a nested prim appends its name to the parent primChildren
adding an attribute or relationship appends its name to the parent prim propertyChildren
attribute and relationship specs with the same parent and name collide because they share the same property path
property specs are leaf specs
active=false must not remove a spec at the layer-storage level
